bull.b: Sustained ARR growth and rapid SaaS ARR expansion materially improve recurring revenue quality and predictability. A larger SaaS base increases customer stickiness and upsell potential, supporting scalable revenue and operating-leverage improvements over the next several quarters.

The shift to positive operating and free cash flow alongside margin expansion provides durable financial flexibility. Improved cash generation can fund product investment, reduce reliance on external financing, and supports the company's ability to sustain operating improvements through economic cycles.

Strategic product innovation targeting nonhuman identities combined with hyperscaler and security partner integrations expands addressable market and creates a tougher-to-replicate go-to-market motion. This strengthens competitive positioning and supports sustained net-new ARR growth if execution holds.

bear.b: Despite recent margin progress, continuing large net and operating losses indicate the business has not yet consistently converted revenue into sustainable earnings. Persistent losses constrain reinvestment flexibility and leave outcomes sensitive to execution and margin recovery over coming quarters.

Prior periods of negative equity and high leverage signal past capital instability. Even with low current leverage, historical swings raise the risk that future funding needs or adverse conditions could pressure the balance sheet or require dilutive or costly financing decisions.

bear.t: Execution Risk On Migrations & New Products
bear.b: Key growth drivers—migration of on‑prem ARR and monetization of Agentic/emerging products—are early-stage and timing-sensitive. If migrations or Agentic monetization lag, revenue and margin improvement could decelerate, making near-term progress fragile and execution-dependent.
